messages.content: summarize this "As answers for mapping questions, it has been found a small number of publications in the earlier years with a significant increase in the studies from 2009, which can be related to the establishment of the symposium on SBSE.Harman and Zhang were the most publishers in this area and “Next Release Problem” by Bagnall () was the most cited paper by the studies analyzed in the proposed review. Additionally, the main forums of accepting publications in this area between 23 symposiums, conferences and journals is SSBSE (Symposium on Search Based Software Engineering)."

The number of publications related to mapping questions has increased significantly since 2009, coinciding with the establishment of the symposium on SBSE. Harman and Zhang were the most active publishers in this field, and the most cited paper in the reviewed studies was "Next Release Problem" by Bagnall. The primary platform for publishing in this area is the SSBSE, which hosts 23 symposiums, conferences, and journals.

write this in academic way "The number of publications related to mapping questions has increased significantly since 2009, coinciding with the establishment of the symposium on SBSE. Harman and Zhang were the most active publishers in this field. The primary platform for publishing in this area is the SSBSE"

The volume of scholarly publications focusing on mapping questions has notably surged since 2009, aligning with the advent of the symposium on search-based software engineering (SBSE). Predominantly, Harman and Zhang have emerged as leading contributors within this domain. Hence, the Symposium on Search-Based Software Engineering (SSBSE) represents the principal forum for disseminating research findings in this particular realm.

The number of publications related to mapping questions has witnessed a considerable increase since 2009, which can be attributed to the establishment of the symposium on search-based software engineering (SBSE). Moreover, Zhang and Harman have emerged as the most prolific publishers in this specific domain. Furthermore, findings indicate that the primary platform for publishing in this area is the Symposium on Search-Based Software Engineering (SSBSE), with seven papers followed by JISWT with six papers.

The publication count concerning mapping questions has experienced a notable rise since 2009, likely attributed to the introduction of the SBSE symposium. Zhang and Harman emerged as prominent contributors in this field. The SSBSE serves as the principal platform for publishing, with seven papers, while JISWT follows closely with six publications.

proofreading "The systematic review findings reported three different problems were addressed in software requirement selection which are single-objective (the most problem formulation in the area), multiple objective and experiments with both. Researchers tend to advance towards using a multi-objective formulation. The adoption of this approach aims to achieve more precise results, considering that software engineering problems in the real world often involve multiple competing objectives. Methods used are meta-heuristics (which is significantly the most commonly applied method), exact search techniques, hybrid methods and a new formulation. Multiple research techniques were used to obtain solutions for the process was demonstrated and NSGA-II is the most algorithm used. However, the authors discuss interesting results by comparing techniques and showing their benefits. "

The systematic review revealed that software requirement selection addressed three distinct problems: single-objective, multiple-objective, and experiments involving both approaches. Among these, single-objective problem formulation emerged as the most prevalent in the field. However, researchers are increasingly inclined towards adopting a multi-objective formulation to obtain more precise results, as real-world software engineering problems often entail multiple competing objectives. The methods employed in this area encompass meta-heuristics (which notably represent the most commonly applied approach), exact search techniques, hybrid methods, and a novel formulation. Various research techniques were utilized to derive solutions for the process, with NSGA-II identified as the most frequently used algorithm. Nevertheless, the authors engage in a discussion of intriguing results obtained by comparing techniques and highlighting their respective advantages.

write this in academic way "The systematic review revealed three problems were addressed in software requirements selection and prioritization: single objective (the most problem formulation in the field), multiple objective which researchers are tending toward this approach to obtaining better results for real world problems and experiments involving both approaches. The methods employed in this area include significant usage of meta-heuristics compared with other methods which are exact search techniques, hybrid methods and a new formulation. Additionally, NSGA-II considered as the most frequently used algorithm in this area. However, the authors discuss the presented technique with their benefits."

The systematic review conducted indicated that software requirements selection and prioritization encompassed three distinct problem categories: single objective, multiple objective, and experiments combining both approaches. Notably, single objective formulation emerged as the prevailing methodology in the field, although researchers are increasingly gravitating towards adopting a multi-objective approach to yield enhanced results for real-world scenarios. Within this domain, the employed methods primarily consisted of meta-heuristics, which enjoyed substantial utilization in comparison to alternative strategies such as exact search techniques, hybrid methods, and a novel formulation. Furthermore, NSGA-II emerged as the algorithm most frequently employed within this framework. It is worth mentioning that the authors examined and discussed the presented techniques alongside their respective benefits.

The systematic review revealed that software requirements selection and prioritization encompassed three problem categories: single objective, multiple objective, and a combination of both. While single objective formulation was the dominant approach, researchers are increasingly adopting a multi-objective approach for improved results in real-world scenarios. Meta-heuristics were the most widely used methods, surpassing alternative strategies like exact search techniques, hybrid methods, and a new formulation. The algorithm most frequently employed in this context was NSGA-II. The authors thoroughly examined and discussed the presented techniques, highlighting their respective advantages.
paraphrase "While single objective was the dominant approach in the time of the study, researchers increasingly adopted a multi-objective approach for more precise results in real-world problems. "

During the study, it was observed that the single objective approach held prominence. However, researchers progressively embraced a multi-objective approach to achieve greater precision in addressing real-world problems.
summarize "Moreover, the most used methods were meta-heuristic compared with alternative methods: exact search techniques, hybrid methods and a new formulation. "
Furthermore, meta-heuristic methods were found to be the most commonly utilized in comparison to alternative approaches such as exact search techniques, hybrid methods, and a novel formulation.

summarize "Furthermore, the suggested studies involved a range of data types, including real data in approximately 50% of the papers, industrial and synthetic data. The scale of experiments conducted varied, with a range from small data to a dataset. "

In addition, the studies recommended in the review encompassed a diverse range of data types, with approximately 50% of the papers utilizing real data, as well as industrial and synthetic data. The scale of experiments conducted varied significantly, ranging from small datasets to larger ones.

summarize "This analysis represents trends and potentials as well as the strengths and limitations when facing challenging tasks. To enhance the reliability of experiments, it is recommended in the study to incorporate a set of functional and non-functional requirements and involve new restrictions closely related to the real-world context. Moreover, the research suggests exploring robust optimization techniques to include risks and uncertainties in the models. Additionally, involving user judgment in the process where the study acknowledges the potential for improved outcomes. "

This analysis provides insights into trends, potentials, strengths, and limitations when tackling challenging tasks. To improve the reliability of experiments, the study recommends incorporating both functional and non-functional requirements, as well as introducing new constraints that closely align with real-world contexts. Furthermore, researchers propose exploring robust optimization techniques to account for risks and uncertainties within the models. Additionally, involving user judgment in the process is recognized as having the potential to enhance outcomes.

Acknowledging the potential for improved outcomes, the study suggests the inclusion of user judgment in the process.
